programme.title: Ride
programme.desc: Struggling actor James pays his bills by driving for a ride-sharing service in Los Angeles. His night soon takes a shocking turn when Bruno, armed with a gun, takes James and a woman on a terrifying, white-knuckle trip that spirals out of control.

programme.title: VFW
programme.desc: A typical night for veterans at a VFW turns into a battle for survival when a desperate teen runs into the bar. When a gang of punks come looking for her, the vets use every weapon at their disposal to protect themselves from an unrelenting attack.

programme.title: Beneath the Darkness
programme.desc: Teenagers discover a terrible secret when they break into the home of a mortician whose wife died two years earlier.

programme.title: The Postcard Killings
programme.desc: A New York detective investigates the death of his daughter, who was murdered while on her honeymoon in London, the victim of an apparent serial killer.

programme.title: Baskin
programme.desc: Policemen (Gorkem Kasal, Ergun Kuyucu) face a night of horror in a subterranean labyrinth after answering a distress call.

programme.title: No Man's Land
programme.desc: When a vigilante border patrol turns fatal, a man flees on horseback to Mexico, seeking forgiveness from the victim's father.

programme.title: Brawl in Cell Block 99
programme.desc: A desperate former boxer takes work as a drug courier and winds up in a jail where his enemies force him to commit acts of violence.

programme.title: Desolation
programme.desc: When a widow ventures into a remote wilderness area with her son and best friend to spread her husband's ashes, a mysterious hiker begins dogging them and forces them to confront their deepest fears.

programme.title: Grabbers
programme.desc: Residents of an island off the coast of Ireland learn that the only way to survive an invasion of blood-sucking aliens is to stay drunk.

programme.title: Revenge
programme.desc: A shocking act leaves a woman brutalized and left for dead by three sleazy men. Unfortunately for her assailants, she survives and soon begins a relentless quest for bloody revenge.

programme.title: Extraterrestrial
programme.desc: Malevolent aliens interrupt five collegians' vacation at a remote woodland cabin.

programme.title: Prisoners of the Ghostland
programme.desc: In Samurai Town, a ruthless bank robber gets sprung from jail by a warlord whose adopted granddaughter has gone missing. Strapped into a leather suit that will self-destruct in five days, the bandit sets off on a journey to find the young woman.

programme.title: Spare Parts
programme.desc: Weapons attached to one arm, four young women must fight like gladiators as a bloodthirsty crowd cheers them on.

programme.title: Welcome to Willits
programme.desc: Locals in the California woods are attacked by supernatural creatures, and when the situation escalates into carnage, a farmer struggles to defend his family.

programme.title: Radioflash
programme.desc: In a dystopian future, a tech-savvy teenager and her father flee the city and seek refuge with her grandfather deep in the mountains of the Pacific Northwest.

programme.title: Exam
programme.desc: Eight candidates cooperate in order to secure a job with a prestigious company.

programme.title: A Lonely Place to Die
programme.desc: Five mountaineers stumble into a vicious kidnapping scheme after they rescue a child they find buried alive in the Scottish Highlands.

programme.title: Re: Born
programme.desc: A legendary soldier with a mysterious past decides to unleash his inner beast to stand up for what he cares about.

programme.title: Into the Ashes
programme.desc: Nick is an ex-con who's adjusting to a new job, a new wife and some semblance of normalcy in rural Alabama. When vengeful former associates track him down, he's soon forced back into the desperate and violent life that he thought he left behind.

programme.title: Nitram
programme.desc: Living a life of isolation and frustration, a young man develops an unexpected friendship with a reclusive heiress. When that relationship meets its tragic end, his loneliness and anger culminates into the most nihilistic and heinous of acts.

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Venice
programme.desc: To get his dog back, private eye Steve Ford forges an unlikely alliance with Spyder, the leader of a local gang. With help from his best friend, Steve pulls out the big guns to retrieve Spyder's missing cash and cocaine and save his beloved pooch.

programme.title: Letters from Baghdad
programme.desc: The extraordinary and dramatic story of Gertrude Bell, the most powerful woman in the British Empire in her day. She shaped the modern Middle East after World War I in ways that still reverberate today. More influential than her friend and colleague Lawrence of Arabia, Bell helped draw the borders of Iraq and established the Iraq Museum. Using never-seen-before footage of the region, the film chronicles Bell's extraordinary journey into both the uncharted Arabian desert and the inner sanctum of British male colonial power. With unique access to documents from the Iraq National Library and Archive and Gertrude Bell's own 1,600 letters, the story is told entirely in the words of the players of the day, excerpted verbatim from intimate letters, private diaries and secret communiques. It is a unique look at both a remarkable woman and the tangled history of Iraq.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches Havant, a town that rapidly expanded after the Second World War and attracted new manufacturing, including a factory making a new and exciting toy – Scalextric. Further north, at Rowlands Castle, Michael visits Britain’s oldest commercial vineyard, Hambledon, established in 1952. Crossing the county border into Surrey, Michael heads to Haslemere, the surprise birthplace of a musical phenomenon – the plastic recorder. And the last stop on this leg of Michael’s journey is Guildford, where he discovers a cutting-edge facility at the forefront of the British space industry.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael begins the final leg of his journey at the Transport Research Laboratory in Wokingham, where they have been keeping Britain’s roads safe for 90 years. From Reading, Michael boards a new Elizabeth Line train to Southall, where he visits the largest gurdwara outside India. Continuing on the Elizabeth Line to Heathrow Airport, Michael hears how it began as a private aerodrome. He is given special access to an enormous concrete box beneath Terminal 5, takes an eerie walk around the mothballed Terminal 1 building and ends his tour atop the iconic 87.5 metre control tower.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: The real side of boating with Robbie Cumming. On the last leg of his epic journey across the north of England, Robbie tackles the fast-flowing River Ouse and is forced to make an emergency stop. His destination is Ripon Basin at the outer reaches of our inland waterways.

programme.title: Mysterious Origins of Insects
programme.desc: Insects are vital to life on our planet, yet almost 40 per cent of known species are in danger of extinction. This film explores the eye-opening realm of these creatures to better understand how they evolved. Travelling from the rain forests of French Guiana to the Arctic Ocean, scientists are using traditional and cutting-edge techniques to examine both modern insects and the fossil record in their search for a single, common insect ancestor.

programme.title: The Nazis: A Warning from History
programme.desc: After the Battle of Stalingrad in the autumn of 1942 and the winter of 1943, the German people experienced nothing but disaster. So why, when the war seemed lost, did the Nazis fight on? This programme examines why Germany had to suffer so much, and in her suffering inflict destruction on countless others. Between July 1944 and May 1945, more Germans would perish than in the previous four years of the war put together. The film shows how fear and hatred of Bolshevism drove many Germans to fight to the bitter end. The extent to which Germany had also become a dramatically racist country also played a part. A former member of the Hitler Youth reveals why he approved of the brutal treatment of Polish forced workers in Germany, and a former slave worker at the IG Farben concentration camp at Auschwitz tells his dramatic story. Germans who lived in the former eastern bloc also talk openly about the whirlwind of death and destruction unleashed by the collapse of the Nazi regime. Their stories include a dramatic eyewitness account of when more than 900 inhabitants of a German village committed suicide by drowning rather than risk facing the occupying Soviet army.

programme.title: Afoot Again in the Past
programme.desc: Lucinda Lambton investigates the history of Castle Leslie in Ireland.

programme.title: Our Friends in the North
programme.desc: On the night of the 1987 hurricane, Nicky leaves Mary. Tosker and Elaine, broken by the stock market crash, find comfort in each other.

programme.title: Our Friends in the North
programme.desc: 1995: Nicky goes back to his roots and to Mary. Geordie has become an alcoholic, and Tosker realises his dream of a floating nightclub.

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Could alien life exist closer than we think - perhaps right next door? The team investigate evidence that suggests the search for life beyond Earth may lie within our own solar system.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Jump on board with narrowboater Robbie Cumming as he explores the Victorian village of Saltaire in West Yorkshire on the Leeds and Liverpool Canal, moors up in Leeds Dock, and goes in search of an abandoned waterway on the Aire and Calder Navigation.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: The real side of boating with Robbie Cumming. On the last leg of his epic journey across the north of England, Robbie tackles the fast-flowing River Ouse and is forced to make an emergency stop. His destination is Ripon Basin at the outer reaches of our inland waterways.

programme.title: This is BBC Four

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo boards the West Highland Line to begin a railway journey across Scotland’s central belt, from the Arrochar Alps to the Loch of the Lowes. At the head of Loch Long, Michael meets a legendary octogenarian munro-bagger. In Balloch, on the shores of Loch Lomond, he joins passionate volunteers working to restore an iconic 1950s paddle steamer. In Glasgow, Michael rides the 'Clockwork Orange', the third oldest underground rail system in the world, to Govan and the shipyards of the River Clyde. And at Kelvinbridge, Michael hears how the chicken tikka masala was created for the Glaswegian palate by an immigrant chef from Lahore.

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the Cornish fishing village of Port Isaac. Behind the quaint facade lies something far more gritty - a place where people exploited a range of natural resources, on land and at sea, to make a living and find profits far beyond Britain's shores.

programme.title: Eiffel Tower: Building the Impossible
programme.desc: A majestic presence, towering over Paris for more than 130 years, the Eiffel Tower is an iconic symbol of 19th-century progress and grandeur. When it was built for the 1889 Paris world’s fair, its creator, Gustave Eiffel, was at the height of his fame and ready to take on the dream of every engineer and architect - to build the world’s tallest man-made structure. He had just two years, two months and five days to erect his 300-metre iron colossus. Drawing on three decades of civil engineering innovation and works across the globe, Eiffel, a magician of iron building, was perfectly poised for this extraordinary feat.

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of a fiercely independent Hollywood star who left it all behind to live life on her own terms, revealing a haunting, resilient journey of art, identity and authenticity in the shadow of one of cinema’s most iconic roles.

programme.title: Vertigo
programme.desc: Suddenly dangerously disorientated by heights, detective John Ferguson resigns from the San Francisco police and is reluctant when asked by an old college friend to trail the man's wife, Madeleine, whose behaviour is causing concern. But John gradually becomes obsessed with the troubled woman and increasingly intervenes in her life. Classic Hitchcock psychological thriller.

programme.title: Hitchcock at the NFT
programme.desc: First broadcast in 1969. In his 70th year, Alfred Hitchcock came to the National Film Theatre in London to talk to fellow director Bryan Forbes and to answer questions from an audience of film enthusiasts. With scenes from Blackmail (1929), The Lady Vanishes (1938), Psycho (1960), The Birds (1963) and Torn Curtain (1966).

programme.title: Linda Ronstadt: The Sound of My Voice
programme.desc: Musical biography of Linda Ronstadt, one of the most successful and versatile female singers of the 20th century - and one of the most successful recording artists of all time. It tells Linda's story through her own words and music, and by such professional colleagues as Dolly Parton, Emmylou Harris, Bonnie Raitt, Jackson Browne, Aaron Neville and many more.

programme.title: Sisters in Country: Dolly, Linda and Emmylou
programme.desc: How Dolly Parton, Linda Ronstadt and Emmylou Harris's careers took off in the 1970s with distinct takes on country, but how they ended up uniting as close harmony singers on Trio.

programme.title: The Climate Question
programme.desc: A global programme that reflects the variety of takes on climate change, how best to understand it and the world's attempts to avert it, temper it or adapt to it. It is not about questioning whether climate change is happening, it's about finding the best ways to respond to it. This is sharp-edged, analytical inquiry. Hard scrutiny, touched with a sense of adventure and discovery, and where we can find it, hope. It includes stories from across the world on why we find it so hard to save our own planet, and how we might change that.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: Nigel Farage has accused parts of the British media of mounting a politically-motivated campaign against him after reports about his financial interests prompted an investigation by Parliament's Standards Commissioner. Gabriel Pogrund, editor of the Insight investigations team at The Sunday Times, outlines the reporting behind their story, while journalist Michael Crick and Brendan O'Neill of Spiked discuss the political and journalistic issues. A High Court judgment has dismissed all claims brought by Prince Harry and six other claimants against Associated Newspapers over allegations that journalists used unlawful methods to obtain information. The ruling marks a significant moment in the long-running legal and political legacy of Britain's phone-hacking and press intrusion scandals. Susan Aslan, media lawyer, outlines the legal significance of the judgment.

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: For 60 years, prides of lions have ruled over the park, often with powerful matriarchs at their heart, keeping everyone in order. But with the loss of one of the pride’s oldest females last year, that balance is beginning to shift. When a pride loses its queen, another must step up. Now the keepers have a challenge on their hands: finding out who will replace her. Will an overnight camera trap capture any evidence of succession? Or could rival leaders reveal themselves when a pride’s territory is challenged with bedding from a neighbouring group? As part of the anniversary celebrations, Ben and Kate revisit the story of Newt the wallaby. Rejected by his mother as a joey and struggling to survive, he faced an uncertain future until the keepers stepped in to hand-rear him. Elsewhere, Ben treats Anne the elephant to one of her favourite snacks, while Megan McCubbin checks on the koalas during breeding season and discovers more about one of the park's lesser-known forest dwellers, the binturong.

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: When a child goes missing, police in Newcastle have few leads to go on. But as the weeks pass by, they’re increasingly concerned about the boy. They have a suspect but not enough evidence to prove anything. Eventually, a torn-up cheque gives them their moment of proof – as the case takes an even darker turn. Also, officers in London get a tip-off about a drugs factory. After a long surveillance operation, they finally uncover a lab making amphetamines underneath a pub.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: A forensic archaeologist locates the body of a teenage girl buried in a cellar, and an expert in scanning technology sends a homemade gun maker to jail.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin follows progress on a project to create student accommodation in Liverpool, which could net the developers a cool £2m profit, while developers in Snodland and Normanton face challenges bringing unloved homes back to life.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and experts Colin Young and Raj Bisram are in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, to go shopping for antiques with the red and blue teams. They are hoping for big profits when they head to the auction in Norfolk. Eric takes a trip down the road to the Harlow Museum to see an amazing collection of rare bicycles from around the world.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: It’s Cup Match weekend, Bermuda’s biggest holiday, and the island is in full swing. At the national cricket ground, Inspector Karema Flood and DC Renay Rock keep a close watch as thousands pour in over two high-energy days of sport. With soaring temperatures, alcohol flowing and emotions running high, the atmosphere quickly shifts as crowds gather for the trophy presentation. Out on the water, Colour Sergeant Tyler Smith and Corporal Neville Vanderpool patrol choppy seas and crowded channels. Missing lifejackets, drifting raft-ups and risky manoeuvres keep them busy until one boat’s journey under a bridge raises serious questions about judgement and responsibility. At Horseshoe Bay’s Beach Fest, officers prepare early for what’s to come. As the beach fills to capacity, PCs Adam Thompson, Melissa Butler, Ayesha Akbar and Sergeant Tavin Trott deal with everything from minor disputes to heat-related incidents – along with joining in the revelry. When a visitor’s mobile phone goes missing in the packed crowds, the search becomes a test of everyone's patience and persistence.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Australia
programme.desc: Rick Stein heads to the Central Coast on Sydney’s northern fringes for a taste of Australia's outdoor lifestyle.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: It’s a first for Escape to the Country as Jules Hudson heads to the Isle of Man. He’s helping a retired nurse from Lincolnshire who has been holidaying on the island for over 70 years. So, the big question is, who is really showing whom around? With a £275,000 budget, they explore charming homes and soak up the highlights, from the harbour town of Peel to the mighty Great Laxey Wheel. There’s even a trip on the Snaefell Mountain Railway to enjoy the breathtaking views from the top. Jules also meets an enthusiastic gardener who took the leap and moved to the island from the USA, despite never having heard of it before.

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presides as members of the public with interesting items to sell come face to face with five dealers who bid against each other to try to bag themselves a bargain. Before facing the dealers, each seller has their item valued by auctioneer Simon Bower. Simon also gives them top tips on the history and key characteristics of their piece, so that when they enter the bidding room, they have all the information they need to try to haggle the best possible price from the dealers. All the dealers are spending their own money but have no idea what the sellers have brought in.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Auctioneer Briony Harford and expert restorer JJ Chalmers are called in to help married couple Emma and Jay downsize their home and raise funds for a once-in-a-lifetime adventure in their camper van. Following the recent loss of Emma’s adventurous mother, and inspired by her love of travel, the couple have decided that life is too short, and now is the time to turn their dream of seeing the world into a reality. They’ve already got the camper van, but now they need to quit their jobs and downsize their home. After inheriting hoards of family belongings over the years, their house is full of remarkable items, including royal memorabilia linked to Emma’s late father, an accountant to King Charles III. It all needs to go if their unconventional ambitions are to be fulfilled, and this clear-out will be no small task. Bee unveils a collection of striking artworks, some echoing the swagger of the Starsky and Hutch era, others bearing a more stately provenance, alongside a perfectly chosen silver passport charm. Meanwhile, JJ reimagines a set of far-flung registration plates as a sleek, contemporary storage piece. But when the hammer falls, which treasures will soar highest at auction?

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: In African Village, it's a worrying time for one of the park's endangered African wild dogs. After breaking his leg, Kenya faces a life-changing operation that has never been carried out in the UK before. The procedure offers his best chance of survival, but the risks are significant. And even if the surgery is successful, there is another challenge ahead. African wild dogs depend on the strength of their pack, and nobody knows how Kenya's brothers will react when he returns. As part of the anniversary celebrations, Ben and Kate look back at one of the most challenging periods in the park's history. In 2001, foot-and-mouth disease threatened animal collections across the country, and the team faced an anxious race to protect the animals in their care. Elsewhere, Ben attempts to impress one of the park's most laid-back residents with a bouquet of edible flowers, while Megan McCubbin helps monitor the growing deer herd and joins the keepers for an important health check. We also look back at the time when Hamza attempted to check on the native herons – can he catch a glimpse of these elusive birds?

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: When an organised crime gang start bringing large quantities of drugs into Crewe, police need to crack down on them, but the gang’s methods make it painstaking work to uncover who they are. Surveillance of one man links him to the kingpin, and police are then able to bring down the entire operation. And when burglars force their way into a vicarage and viciously attack people inside, it’s one brave victim's memory of an unusual tattoo that gives officers their moment of proof.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: A forensic image analyst identifies a killer from images in CCTV, and a forensic specialist helps police unravel the truth when a case goes from missing to murder.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion is troubled by issues with damp at two flats in Liverpool. Martel is in Ayrshire, Scotland, where damp is also the order of the day. Jacqui is in Buckinghamshire to see a beautiful period property that needs some love.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley presents a fever-pitched football special from Detling in Kent. Two teams of football fans shop for three items to take to auction, with the help of experts Tim Weeks and Nick Hall. There’s plenty of friendly rivalry as they try to score the best deals, but who will come out as champions? Caroline meets Baz Davison to discuss his collection of retro World Cup 1994 football shirts.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: An island-wide drugs crackdown targets a suspected carfentanyl courier, cannabis plants spark a doorstep confession, and a sniffer dog flags suspicious luggage at the airport.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Australia
programme.desc: Rick Stein retraces the journey he first made as a 19-year-old hitchhiker, travelling up from Sydney towards Queensland on the north coast of New South Wales.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: It’s all go in gorgeous Powys as Jules Hudson helps a couple to swap town life in Oxfordshire for his beloved Wales. On the wish list is space for two dogs, three cats and a garage for a motorbike, which the brand spanking new mystery eco-house might just deliver. For an inspiring change of pace, Jules visits a brilliant local farming initiative that’s helping young people get into agriculture and grow sustainable food for the future.

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presides as members of the public with interesting items to sell come face to face with five dealers who bid against each other to try to bag themselves a bargain. Before facing the dealers, each seller has their item valued by auctioneer Simon Bower. Simon also gives them top tips on the history and key characteristics of their piece, so that when they enter the bidding room, they have all the information they need to try to haggle the best possible price from the dealers. All the dealers are spending their own money but have no idea what the sellers have brought in.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: After many happy years of married life, Cheryl’s beloved husband Pete passed away just a few months ago. A great collector and skilled with his hands, Pete was affectionately known by his family as Mr Fixit. But sadly, after surviving a stroke at a young age, Pete went on to suffer several years of poor health, and many of his ambitious projects remain incomplete. Leaving behind a number of partly restored motor vehicles, as well as an overwhelming amount of potentially valuable toys and novelty items, it’s time for a fresh start. But for Cheryl, who also lost her mum last year, the task of clearing out is more than she can take right now. So, she’s asked auctioneer Izzie Balmer and restorer Robin Johnson to help her. It’s transport galore for Izzie and Robin as they uncover a Velocette bicycle, a tricycle, a boat and even a scratch-build 4x4. Robin turns his specialist skills to an early e-bike, while Izzie hunts for treasures inside. At auction, a Royal Albert County Rose tea, coffee and dinner service wows the crowds, selling for over £600, and all the money raised mounts up to be shared among Pete’s family. A fitting tribute to Mr Fixit.

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Hidden away above the bat cave, conservationists are investigating a worrying decline in one of the park's rare native bat colonies. Numbers have fallen sharply, and there are fears another species may be threatening the next generation. As the team search for answers, they hope to discover whether the colony can recover before more bats are lost. As part of the show's silver anniversary celebrations, Ben and Kate revisit one of the most memorable stories from the past 25 years. As the anniversary celebrations continue, Ben and Kate look back at the time a binturong mysteriously started refusing food, seriously worrying the keepers. The only option is an anaesthetic to try to get to the root of the problem, but with any procedure of this kind there is always a worry that the animal may never wake up. Elsewhere, the keepers celebrate an important milestone as a critically endangered white-backed vulture chick reaches a crucial stage in its development. Megan McCubbin also joins the keepers to weigh the park's three male gorillas. With each weighing more than twice as much as the average adult man, it's no easy task getting them onto the scales.

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A family home is targeted in an arson attack – and two people die. But the matches police found are damp and only give a partial DNA profile. There is nothing to put the main suspects at the scene until an eagle-eyed detective realises there is something wrong with the CCTV footage. Also, police hunting for a burglar who’s stolen hundreds of thousands of pounds' worth of jewellery get their moment of proof from a distinctive piece of clothing.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: A forensic biologist uses modern DNA techniques to solve the cold case murder of Hilda Murrell, and cell site analysis helps place a suspect at the scene of Abdul Deghayes’s killing.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers buy a pair of cottages in north Norfolk and plan to create one stunning family home. Martel meets a developer in North Yorkshire, who puts his DIY skills to the test refurbishing a three-bedroom semi-detached house. In Somerset, Martin tours a tired terrace that has just five weeks to be transformed for new tenants.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine visits Hereford, a city closely linked to the military, to see a collection of Second World War vehicles, equipment and memorabilia. At The Bunker, she meets Colin and Kathryn Taylor, World War II enthusiasts and custodians of this living museum, to learn about the heroic work of the military police, field medics and the famous SAS. From the passenger seat of a restored Jeep and a Humber ambulance, Roo sees how Colin and Kathryn are helping to keep history alive. Over in Leominster, two teams of bargain hunters are on manoeuvres, seeking profitable antiques to take to auction. On team red are twin sisters Jo and Sarah with their expert Charles Hanson. On the blue team are father and daughter Bobby and Jas, with Richard Madley. With £300 in their pockets, Bobby and Jas spot a desktop barometer and thermometer set and decide it’s a fair-weather option for later. Across the antiques centre, Charles think he's seeing double when Jo picks up an art nouveau sculpture that he believes looks just like her! Up in Wrexham, auctioneer Matthew Tong casts a keen eye on the teams’ items before putting them under the hammer.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Hairdresser Mysteries
programme.desc: Hairdresser Lily Petal opens her new salon in the sleepy village of Blossom Vale, finding herself investigating a suspicious death and the theft of a valuable antique.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Australia
programme.desc: Rick travels to Australia's famous holiday hotspot Byron Bay. On his way, he takes a detour to the fishing town of Ballina, just south of the Byron Bay hinterland.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Returning home after eight years of living and working in Abu Dhabi, two teachers want to swap desert sand for the beaches of East Sussex, where Briony May Williams has the keys to what could be the couple’s new home. Keen to get involved in the local community, they visit the Bluebell Railway to see what volunteering opportunities there are on the heritage line.

programme.desc: Nigel Havers presides as members of the public with interesting items to sell come face to face with five dealers who bid against each other to try to bag themselves a bargain. programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Bee and Lauren help Emma downsize a home full of memories after losing her husband. Finding items full of stories in his home library, the family will turn his loss into legacy.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mark puts Lauren to the test, Vicki and Ross face another stumbling block, and Gina attempts to right a wrong.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: The team help the Clarke family, whose home has been overwhelmed by the hobbies they love, leaving little room for everyday life.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is back in the host’s chair for the award-winning comedy panel show, with Lee Mack and David Mitchell returning as the lightning-quick team captains. Each week, a stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up? Sorting the fact from the fiction this week are guest panellists Harry Hill, Kojey Radical, Jill Scott and Stacey Solomon.

programme.title: Ann Droid
programme.desc: Sue’s husband Dave died two years ago, and now her only son, Michael, is moving out – again – to try to fix his marriage – again. But the good news is he has got Sue a surprise to help her live independently: an Ann Droid Z58/100 humanoid care robot. Sue really does not want a robot in her home, especially one that causes chaos straightaway. But is this robot actually the companion she most needs?

programme.title: Austin

programme.title: My Old Ass
programme.desc: In this coming-of-age fable, free-spirited teenager Elliott encounters her wisecracking 39-year-old future self and can’t escape her influence. On the cusp of leaving home for college, Elliott is determined to have one last summer packed with good times. To celebrate her 18th birthday, she boats off to a remote island with her best friends to trip on magic mushrooms. But just when Elliott thinks she’s not feeling a thing, she suddenly finds herself chatting with a woman who claims to be her literal 'old ass', the person she is apparently destined to become in two decades' time! Prodded for advice, Elliott’s future self gives her one major warning: avoid anyone named Chad, which sounds easy enough. But when Elliott meets the very Chad she was supposed to dodge, things get complicated. Elliott starts to open herself to what her future self might have to show her... and vice versa!

programme.desc: Queens from around the world descend on Canada and form girl groups to kick off the battle for the crown. Guest judge is Anjulie.

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: The queens impersonate celebrities and play the iconic Snatch Game, with a twist. Guest jdges are Sarain Fox and Priyanka.

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: This year's last five stand-up hopefuls battle it out in Bangor for a place in Glasgow's grand final.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Relive a hugely dramatic World Cup quarter-final at Lusail Stadium.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: The Netherlands stun reigning champions Spain.

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Relive the 2022 World Cup final between Argentina and France.

programme.title: Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse
programme.desc: A vengeful anomaly on Miles Morales’s Earth that Gwen Stacy is sent to stop means the parted teens reunite – but destructive consequences affect the multiple Spider-being universes from which Miles has been isolated. Animated superhero action comedy sequel.

programme.title: Ludwig
programme.desc: A body falls from the fourth storey of a construction site. Was it just a terrible accident or a perfectly executed murder? John’s team think he’s losing his touch when he latches on to a far-fetched theory and starts pointing fingers. When things don’t quite add up, John begins to question his abilities both at work and at home, whilst Lucy’s frustration at their lack of progress in finding her husband reaches fever pitch.

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda finds out that a big house on her street is owned by an old lady who is planning to move out. So, she inserts herself into her life, hoping to get first refusal on the sale.

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda tries to persuade Felicity to give her the money she needs to buy her dream house. Meanwhile, Fi buys some wheels for her business, which transforms her into a white-van man.

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda hosts a prom pre-party at her flat for Georgie and her friends, but Felicity gets in the way, the make-up designer is terrible, and the limo gets stuck.

programme.title: Pretty Woman
programme.desc: Multimillionaire Edward Lewis gets lost in Los Angeles during a business trip. After he pays Hollywood Boulevard prostitute Vivian to direct him to his hotel, the pair form an unlikely partnership as he hires her to stay with him, but they discover there are significant hurdles to overcome as they try to bridge the gap between their two very different worlds.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: A full rerun of the France v Spain semi-final from Dallas Stadium, Texas, at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Auctioneer James Broad and restorer Saf Fakir travel to Manchester to help Caroline, whose life was turned upside down when her husband Nick was diagnosed with early-onset Alzheimer’s. Now in a care home, Nick’s absence has left Caroline alone in the family home they built together, surrounded by a lifetime of memories. With her sister-in-law Heather by her side, she’s now ready to face the emotional challenge of sorting through Nick's collections. JB uncovers family heirlooms and fascinating finds, from vintage toys and enamel signs to a remarkable collection of sketches by celebrated Manchester artist Harry Rutherford. Meanwhile, Saf works his magic on a well-loved bamboo hall stand and a broken vintage sign that needs some TLC. He’s determined to restore them to their former glory. Every item sold brings Caroline a step closer to easing the cost of Nick’s ongoing care and to finding peace as she begins a new chapter of her own.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Denise Nurse is in the Gloucestershire countryside, helping Charlotte from Croydon in south London find a rural escape with help from her eldest son, Ben. Included in the fantastic mix of properties are character cottages, a spacious new build and an imaginative conversion, offering plenty to tempt her. Taking a break from house hunting, Denise then heads to a local farm to meet some adorable spring lambs before getting stuck into feeding time.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael leaves Kyushu by foot tunnel under the Kanmon straits to arrive on the main island of Honshu at the harbour town of Shimonoseki. Here, he encounters the deadly delicacy of pufferfish. Containing a neurotoxin with no known antidote, only highly trained chefs can prepare the fish. Will Michael give it a try? Travelling eastwards along the Honshu coast, Michael visits Hitachi’s Kasado Works, where the iconic Shinkansen high-speed trains are manufactured. He is thrilled to get inside the factory and see these amazing machines up close. To end this leg, Michael arrives by Shinkansen into Hiroshima, the city devastated by the atomic bomb dropped by US forces on 6th August 1945. Michael crosses the city to visit the Peace Memorial Park, where he meets Keiko Ogura, a bomb survivor who was just eight years old on that fateful day.

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo’s railway adventure through Uzbekistan reaches its finale in the historic city of Bukhara. Amidst the medieval Islamic architecture, Michael goes in search of a centuries-old Jewish community. Stepping inside a 420-year-old synagogue and a family home, he’s introduced to unique customs and traditions, and a fragrant green dish that has made Bukharan Jews famous worldwide. Michael grapples with kurash, an ancient form of upright wrestling once used to select the bravest warriors and now also increasingly popular as a sport for young Uzbek women. Michael’s exploration of Uzbekistan culminates in the vast Kyzylkum Desert, where he experiences the life of nomadic tribes who once roamed its vast sandy plains. His Central Asian journey concludes among the horses and riders who brave all to take part in one of the most dangerous equestrian sports in the world.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall, Jodie Kidd, Jamali Maddix and Suzi Perry take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games kitchen composter?

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents the fourth semi-final of the classic quiz where contenders return to the black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock to win a place in the grand final. The specialist subjects are Frank Zappa, the life and works of Siegfried Sassoon, pioneering British and Irish female aviators, and winners of the Eurovision Song Contest.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Wiltshire farmers Rebecca and Luke rear their cattle, sheep and pigs with the help of their four children. Always aiming to farm as sustainably as they can, selling their award-winning meat at Stroud Farmers’ Market in Gloucestershire is a vital source of income. It’s a long day, but Luke hopes his barbecued burgers and sausages will be hot favourites with hungry customers. Meanwhile, their daughter Esme is on the charm offensive, determined to do her family proud and pocket as many cold meat sales as she can. Self-taught fruit farmers Helen and David have been selling at Stroud for over 20 years. They have ploughed all their efforts into growing over 250 rare fruit varieties in their Gloucestershire orchard. They pick over 40 tonnes of apples each year, which they sell fresh and bottled as juice and homemade cider. Both families rely on Stroud’s weekly farmers’ market for their livelihood, and it’s full-on for five hours to secure a successful day’s takings.

programme.title: The Hidden World of Hospitality with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Tom Kerridge continues to lift the lid on the UK’s hospitality industry, this time turning the spotlight on staffing. Despite a UK-wide recruitment crisis, Tom discovers how some businesses are finding clever ways to build and retain strong, effective teams.

programme.title: Yellowstone: Wildest Winter to Blazing Summer
programme.desc: The story of animals surviving one of the harshest seasonal changes on the planet continues. It is summer, and the Yellowstone beavers have a new challenge. Will the young survive as the river dries up and the colony is forced to move home? As food becomes scarce, wolves have a surprising strategy to keep their pups fed and grizzly bears are unexpected visitors on a cowboy ranch. By midsummer, the hot dry conditions create a new danger - deadly wildfires burn out of control and threaten to engulf a family of great grey owls. 2016 was the hottest year on earth since records began, and across Yellowstone scientists reveal the effects of rising temperatures on the animals that live here.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques series. Flog It! comes from Hopetoun House near Edinburgh. Antiques experts Adam Partridge and Caroline Hawley find some fascinating antiques and collectibles to take to auction. Paul Martin discovers more about Hopetoun House by talking to the people who work and live there.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Eniola Aluko, Phil Daniels, Nikita Kanda and Danny Miller take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball heads to south London to convince charity worker Latoya that gardening could be her secret wellbeing weapon. She is a total beginner, but the science is firmly on her side. Getting hands on with soil can build strength, bolster the immune system and reduce stress. If Latoya is willing to get her hands dirty, she could see her health blossom.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: After being brutally double-crossed by fellow Traitor Harry Clark, Paul Gorton is taking a second swipe at his rival - this time on the hunt to get one up in the world of antiques. The pair are reunited in the Scottish Highlands, vying to be the winner at auction. Aiding and abetting their efforts in Scotland are antiques experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Roo Irvine, and they hit the road in a Morgan 4/4. In the shops, Harry stumbles on a very apt mug, while Paul resorts to unusual tactics in a bid to bump up his profits. But will it pay off?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restore a unique pair of cowboy boots, a piece of plaster holding a loved one's message, a puppet that has lost its limbs and a pioneering film-maker's award.

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the second episode of this two-part series, Simon Reeve travels from the Peloponnese peninsula to the rugged and mountainous north of the country. To learn more about Greece and the Greeks, he meets an extraordinary cast of characters, from a group of rebel monks to conservationists caring for an injured bear cub. Getting behind the picture-postcard image of this beautiful country, he finds out how the Greeks are coming to terms with a seemingly endless crisis.

programme.title: Inside No. 9
programme.desc: Dawn and Mick are hosting their annual Last Night of the Proms party, the perfect antidote to post-Brexit Britain. But not everyone is singing from the same song sheet.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Matt Chorley.

programme.title: The Tech Billionaire Takeover
programme.desc: A new generation of tech billionaires believe their technologies can replace democracy as we know it and upend the global financial system. Matt Shea gets inside access to this new super-elite, visiting the sovereign nations they're building and tracing their growing political connections to the Trump administration. Are these men trying to take over the world?

programme.title: Amityville: An Origin Story
programme.desc: In 1975, when George and Kathy Lutz move into their suburban dream home, a cascade of terrifying, unexplainable events drive them out in just 28 days. Those closest to the terror share their stories.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the final day of this week’s quiz as the action arrives in Bangor. TV presenter Owain Wyn Evans, Olympic champion Greg Rutherford and Blue Peter’s Abby Cook return, and Paul Gorton resumes his role of unreliable tour guide, sharing stories, surprises and suspect claims as the overall winner is finally revealed.

programme.title: Scamanda
programme.desc: Amanda is given a devastating cancer diagnosis. Her community and church rally around her as she inspires thousands more with a blog, bravely chronicling her journey.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Auctioneer Briony Harford and expert restorer JJ Chalmers are called in to help married couple Emma and Jay downsize their home and raise funds for a once-in-a-lifetime adventure in their camper van. Following the recent loss of Emma’s adventurous mother, and inspired by her love of travel, the couple have decided that life is too short, and now is the time to turn their dream of seeing the world into a reality. They’ve already got the camper van, but now they need to quit their jobs and downsize their home. After inheriting hoards of family belongings over the years, their house is full of remarkable items, including royal memorabilia linked to Emma’s late father, an accountant to King Charles III. It all needs to go if their unconventional ambitions are to be fulfilled, and this clear-out will be no small task. Bee unveils a collection of striking artworks, some echoing the swagger of the Starsky and Hutch era, others bearing a more stately provenance, alongside a perfectly chosen silver passport charm. Meanwhile, JJ reimagines a set of far-flung registration plates as a sleek, contemporary storage piece. But when the hammer falls, which treasures will soar highest at auction?

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: It’s chocks away as Christina Trevanion pilots a special episode all about aviation. Among the historic aircraft of the Royal Airforce Museum London, Christina rounds up some deals that really took off at auction and others that had a bumpy ride. In Ardingly, a former flying ace and his daughter plan to soar to victory when they go shopping with Nick Hall, while in Newark, university friends Joel and Oscar hope to land a good deal with a propeller made into a coat rack, until their expert Catherine Southon grounds the idea. In Hendon, Christina learns about the history and heroism of the pilots who flew some of the 74 aircraft on display, and in Bristol, Eric Knowles reminisces about the glamour of supersonic travel from the luxurious interior of a surviving Concorde jet. Meanwhile, Natasha Raskin Sharp is flying high in a far older plane – a restored World War II Spitfire.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Gardeners’ World comes from the very first RHS Badminton Flower Show, set in the beautiful south Gloucestershire countryside. Adam Frost, Rachel De Thame and Ashley Edwards explore the show gardens, young designer gardens, feature gardens, pocket planting and artisan gardens, and seek out the season's must-have plants in the floral marquee. There are also oodles of practical gardening advice and innovative design to inspire you in your garden.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall, Jodie Kidd, Jamali Maddix and Suzi Perry take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games kitchen composter?

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents the fifth semi-final of the classic quiz where contenders return to the black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock to win a place in the grand final. The specialist subjects are Fridtjof Nansen, the record label 4AD, Christina Rossetti and the Jason Bourne films.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents the last semi-final of the classic quiz where contenders return to the black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock to win a place in the grand final. The specialist subjects are Sir Edwin Lutyens, Coldplay, the Karen Pirie novels of Val McDermid and the films of Terry Gilliam.

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The lives of dairy farming couple Jo and Alex revolve around two things: their Jersey Cows and winning at the Ashbourne Show in Derbyshire. They’re returning this year with 11 cows and all the family in tow, determined to hold onto their championship title. Also a show regular is shire horse breeder John, who is here with his daughter Catherine. She has to pick up the reins and go it alone in the competition ring for the very first time while John performs his chair duties for the day and supports her from the sidelines.

programme.title: The Hidden World of Hospitality with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Tom meets trailblazing operators who are risking everything to redefine the British pub, all whilst trying to navigate the cost of living crisis. He visits a rural pub that began a refurbishment during the Covid-19 pandemic, as well as a pair of friends who have created a state-of-the-art brewery and taproom.

programme.title: Land of the Lost Wolves
programme.desc: At a time when wildlife is disappearing across the planet, one animal is making a comeback - the wolf. Wolves were wiped out across much of America, with more than a million wolves estimated to have been shot, poisoned or trapped when European settlers arrived. This enthralling series documents the return of one very special wolf pack to the snowy peaks of Washington's Cascade Mountains - the first to return to the American Northwest in 70 years.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Flog It! comes from Norwich Cathedral in Norfolk. In the great nave, experts Kate Bateman and David Fletcher pick out some choice items to take to auction. Among them are an enamel-topped silver trinket box and a vintage model car. Paul Martin uncovers the meaning of religious graffiti.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Eniola Aluko, Phil Daniels, Nikita Kanda and Danny Miller take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Bristol, distillery manager Luke is relying on caffeine to keep stress at bay, but Clive Myrie has a gentler, smarter swap in mind. If Luke traded his coffee and energy drinks for tea, the benefits could be huge. Studies show that drinking black tea regularly can reduce anxiety, calm the brain and even support heart health. A simple brew with surprisingly powerful perks.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps an aspiring writer who wants to make a dramatic relocation from the frenetic bustle of East London to the rural idylls of Dumfries and Galloway in Scotland. With a budget of £300,000, he’s got his sights set on somewhere isolated that will inspire his creativity. Away from the houses, Alistair heads for the hills hoping to strike it lucky on a Scottish gold rush.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: After the scorching heat of early summer, Monty is working to keep the garden looking its best through the season's challenges. He tackles essential jobs, including cutting back perennials to make room for more colour, tending long grass and pruning step-over apples, while proving there's still time to sow vegetables for delicious late-summer harvests. Best of all, he enjoys some of summer's greatest rewards – lifting the first new potatoes and gathering fragrant sweet peas for the house. Adam Frost discovers a remarkable 2.5-acre walled garden in Surrey packed with hidden gems and unexpected delights. Sue Kent demonstrates how imagination and resourcefulness can transform everyday items into beautiful and productive growing spaces, while in Cornwall, there's a fruit and vegetable professor whose lifelong fascination with pests and diseases is helping gardeners grow healthier crops. Plus a visit to a grower with a striking collection of crassulas, showcasing the extraordinary beauty and diversity of these popular succulents.

programme.title: Golf: The Open
programme.desc: Highlights from the first day of the 154th Open Championship at Royal Birkdale. World number one Scottie Scheffler defends the title he won in emphatically in 2025, and world number two Rory McIlroy looks to add a second Claret Jug to the second Green Jacket he won at the Masters in April. Southport local Tommy Fleetwood will have the support of the home crowd as he aims to land his first major title, fellow Englishman Matt Fitzpatrick hopes to cap a stellar season by becoming the Champion Golfer of the Year, while Justin Rose returns to the venue where he famously burst onto the scene back in 1998.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: ODI Cricket
programme.desc: Highlights from the second ODI of 2026 between England and India.

programme.title: Amityville: An Origin Story
programme.desc: A year before the Lutzes move into 112 Ocean Ave, the DeFeo family is found shot to death under strange circumstances in the same house. The lone survivor, Ronnie DeFeo Jr, is accused of murder, but mob ties and paranormal theories cloud the case.

programme.title: The Big Cases
programme.desc: How the husband of Scotland's longest-serving first minister, Nicola Sturgeon, embezzled £400,000 from the party he ran - using a paper trail of lies to cover up a spending spree on luxury goods, from a motor home to coffee machines and even toilet seats. Peter Murrell was one of the most powerful figures in Scottish politics, operating away from the spotlight as chief executive of Scotland's ruling party. But behind the scenes, police pieced together a scandal - which ended in a dawn raid - involving hundreds of thousands of pounds in missing money. He pleaded guilty, while his estranged wife, the party's former leader, denied all knowledge. From the corridors of Holyrood to the highest ranks of the SNP, loyalties fractured as Mr Murrell's reputation collapsed. With insider testimony, analysis and extraordinary revelations from the case, this is a story of power, money and betrayal at the heart of modern Scottish politics.

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Alan watches on as crewmates Tracey and Lauren help his wife Jean from the floor of their caravan. Alarm bells ring as checks reveal she is suffering from life-threatening sepsis.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: After many happy years of married life, Cheryl’s beloved husband Pete passed away just a few months ago. A great collector and skilled with his hands, Pete was affectionately known by his family as Mr Fixit. But sadly, after surviving a stroke at a young age, Pete went on to suffer several years of poor health, and many of his ambitious projects remain incomplete. Leaving behind a number of partly restored motor vehicles, as well as an overwhelming amount of potentially valuable toys and novelty items, it’s time for a fresh start. But for Cheryl, who also lost her mum last year, the task of clearing out is more than she can take right now. So, she’s asked auctioneer Izzie Balmer and restorer Robin Johnson to help her. It’s transport galore for Izzie and Robin as they uncover a Velocette bicycle, a tricycle, a boat and even a scratch-build 4x4. Robin turns his specialist skills to an early e-bike, while Izzie hunts for treasures inside. At auction, a Royal Albert County Rose tea, coffee and dinner service wows the crowds, selling for over £600, and all the money raised mounts up to be shared among Pete’s family. A fitting tribute to Mr Fixit.

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben and Kate celebrate 25 years of friendship, animal stories and unforgettable moments on Animal Park, and revisit the extraordinary life of lioness Malaika.

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: Marcus wants to impress the locals with a French version of the classic Sunday roast - but first, he needs the perfect poulet. He also gives a masterclass on how to portion a whole chicken and tries to find the best wine to accompany the dish. Later, Marcus finds out how the French enjoy their take on the sandwich. Baguette in hand, he hits the market stalls looking for inspiration.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: Nick Robinson in conversation with people who influence our political thinking.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall, Jodie Kidd, Jamali Maddix and Suzi Perry take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games kitchen composter?

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents the grand final of the classic quiz where six contenders return to the black chair in a bid to become the next Mastermind champion. The specialist subjects are British and Irish Lions tours in the 21st century, Princess Margaret, the Rocky films, Bletchley Park during the Second World War, Augustus Caesar and the BBC sitcom Extras.

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Sheep farming couple Tim and Tanya have breathed new life into their Cotswold farm, ensuring that Tim’s young son has the chance to take on a viable business in the future. Their most ambitious venture was three years in the making: they have recently launched the UK’s first sheep’s milk vodka. However, at £60 per bottle, the challenge will be to sell it. Tim and Tanya have high hopes that they can reel in potential buyers for their artisan spirit at Blenheim Palace Food Festival, even before lunchtime! Meanwhile, Wye Valley-based brothers Matt and Kit are working round the clock collecting honey from 150 beehives and making it into sparkling mead. As a young family business, they rely on their own teamwork to make the dream work. They hope bottles will fly off their stall once people sample their modern take on this ancient drink. On top of that, creating a buzz at this prestigious event could bring them closer to landing a lucrative deal.

programme.title: The Hidden World of Hospitality with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Tom serves up more revelations from the world of hospitality. This time, his attention turns to street food, which is experiencing something of a boom when the industry is struggling. He meets the founders of a street food collective that brings togeter local traders with DJs and live music; an authentic Thai food outfit taking the leap to bricks and mortar; and a barbecue smokehouse in Cornwall that resolutely stays open all year round. And in Marlow, it’s the opening night of Tom’s own food festival, Pub in the Park.

programme.title: Land of the Lost Wolves
programme.desc: After spending four weeks camping out in the bitterly cold Cascade Mountains, wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan and a team of wolf experts discover that most of the pack have been killed by locals who live by the motto 'shoot, shovel and shut up'. But have any of the pack escaped unharmed? As the team chase exciting new leads, there is a glimmer of hope on the horizon.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: This episode comes from the RAF Museum at Hendon in London. Antiques experts Michael Baggott and Mark Stacey find some real gems among the bags and boxes to take to auction. There's a nautilus shell that lights up the saleroom and some Superman film memorabilia that goes sky high. And Paul Martin explores the history of one of Britain's most famous tourist attractions; Highgate cemetery.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Eniola Aluko, Phil Daniels, Nikita Kanda and Danny Miller take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Ever wonder if doom-scrolling is draining your joy? You’re not alone. The science says cutting smartphone use to just two hours a day could seriously boost your wellbeing – but actually doing it is another story. So Zoe Ball is in Kettering to share with busy beautician Lucie some clever digital detox tricks that could improve family life, reduce stress and even boost her brain power. Less scrolling, more living.

programme.title: BBC Proms
programme.desc: The world’s greatest classical music festival gets under way live from the Royal Albert Hall, with works by Copland, Gershwin and Ravel. Petroc Trelawny and Georgia Mann guide us through a night of spectacular music-making to kick off the summer's Proms season. Copland’s landmark Fanfare for the Common Man opens, followed by Gershwin’s ever-popular An American in Paris, and Ravel’s bluesy Piano Concerto, performed by piano superstar Yunchan Lim. A world premiere by Josephine Stephenson starts the second half, followed by Finzi’s ode to the patron saint of music – For St Cecilia – with tenor Thomas Atkins. Dalia Stasevska conducts the BBC Symphony Orchestra, BBC Singers and BBC Symphony Chorus.

programme.title: Golf: The Open
programme.desc: Highlights from the second day of the 154th Open Championship at Royal Birkdale. World number one Scottie Scheffler defends the title he won in emphatically in 2025, and world number two Rory McIlroy looks to add a second Claret Jug to the second Green Jacket he won at the Masters in April. Southport local Tommy Fleetwood will have the support of the home crowd as he aims to land his first major title, fellow Englishman Matt Fitzpatrick hopes to cap a stellar season by becoming the Champion Golfer of the Year, while Justin Rose returns to the venue where he famously burst onto the scene back in 1998.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: Insomnia
programme.desc: Psychological thriller. Detective Will Dormer, a veteran of the Los Angeles Police Department, temporarily escapes an internal affairs investigation that may ruin his career by travelling to Nightmute, Alaska, to investigate the murder of a 17-year-old girl. Evidence mounts up against a reclusive novelist, but the perpetual daylight of the summer months within the Arctic Circle wreaks sleep-depriving havoc on the detective's body clock and threatens his judgement.

programme.title: Morningside
programme.desc: In the run-down Toronto district of Scarborough, the lives of assorted residents are linked by the Morningside Community Centre, closing to be replaced by condominiums as part of encroaching gentrification. Personal struggles, relationships, jealousies and societal challenges are explored in a place where senseless gun violence is also on the rise. Tragedy is inevitable.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Tune every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: An all-night gaming session turns the Farmer and Bitzer into sleep-deprived zombies the next day. Shaun takes the chance to change Bitzer’s checklist to suit himself, but he manages to miss the final item: shearing time!

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: When secret information is leaked from HQ, all eyes fall on Colonel K. Is it possible he is a traitor, or is there something suspicious about his moustache?

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The forest has run out of bath sponges and is becoming very stinky. Their latest order never arrived, and so the heroes must quest under the sea to Merhaven to collect them. However, Hoofius, whose singing stinks worse than his armpits, decides to enter the annual Merhaven Song Contest and accidentally causes a simple quest to collect bath sponges to turn into something much more perilous.

Thinking they had just come for a football masterclass, what will they make of an England legend paying them a surprise visit?

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds an electrode that controls a robot, and the Lemmings plot to take it.

programme.title: Duck and Frog

programme.desc: Gross Alert! Exploring the weird and wonderful world of medicine. From sneezes and snot to pimples and poo, nothing escapes the Doctors' crazy experiments!

programme.desc: Steve is on a mission to unearth the sneaky dinosaurs that were far deadlier than they first appeared! We're learning new things about even the most notorious dinosaurs, and first up, Steve meets Sinornithosaurus - a dinosaur hiding a deadly dental secret. He then races a velociraptor in a climbing challenge, with a surprising result. Finally, Steve puts Argentinasaurus to the ultimate test to see how earth-shattering its stamp really was.

programme.title: Horrible Science
programme.desc: Revolting space travel, evolving cows, pet mushrooms, and Einstein watches a car on a trampoline.

programme.desc: The One Zoo Three brothers join big-cat keeper Briony at the cat sanctuary, where all the animals are limbering up for a very special day of sport. Rare Amur leopard Xizi tackles a specially prepared assault course, while snow leopard Khumbhu is put through his paces as he begins training for a marathon move. Meanwhile, Jua the serval baffles the brothers with her aerial acrobatics in the high jump, and Yazi the puma sinks her teeth into a game of basketball. Cam puts his foot in it while cleaning out Aquarius the fishing cat’s stinky diving pool, and Tyler puts his super-sensitive sniff to the test as he tries to guess Whose Poo? And the boys look back on their African adventure, where they help a conservation project in Kenya track down lions and help protect them in the wild.

programme.title: Gym Stars

programme.title: Cooking Buddies
programme.desc: Buddy and Conker get a call for help from Jervis in Northern Ireland, a brilliant basketball player who wants a slam-dunk meal for his mum to take to work. She’s always doing things for the family, so he wants to pop something in her lunch box to make her smile! Buddy knows the power of a tasty meal, so he teaches Jervis to make mini quiches that will fit easily in her packed lunch. They make the pastry from scratch, and because they’re homemade, choose whatever fillings Jervis and his mum would like best! Join them as they take time out from the kitchen to shoot some hoops and Buddy learns the essential skills of basketball; controlling the ball, dribbling, defending and shooting. SCOOOORE! Join Buddy, Conker and Jervis as they create super-tasty lunchtime bites in this slam dunk of an episode of Cooking Buddies. You can get all of Buddy’s recipes, and loads more, at bbc.co.uk/food.

programme.desc: If you thought that dinosaurs were the most dangerous things in the Jurassic, think again! There was actually something way worse and far more deadly! Meanwhile, Bactrian camels Mo and Navas display some serious suction with their super-slurping, and Cletus the dwarf crocodile arrives at the zoo ready to flex his pecs. Buddy shows her how to make healthy chicken breasts, served with fresh pasta to give them loads of energy, cutting out their own shapes in a cool hack to personalise their pasta and bring it all together with a delicious homemade pesto with added peas – it’s a chicken pea-sto pasta! In a handbrake turn, they leave the kitchen and head to the BMX racetrack where Lakey teaches Buddy how to set off properly at the start of a race, how to squash and pop on the track and to corner properly to shave off time. Join Buddy, Conker and Lakey in a race to the finish line as they get first prize in the kitchen! You can get all of Buddy’s recipes, and loads more, at bbc.co.uk/food.

Scally the African spotted eagle owl’s home gets a new makeover, and the brothers make a bit of a splash refreshing the penguins' pool. The otters are cooling down thanks to a clever recycled shower, and Aaron springs into action at the duck pond to help clean the water fountain. The brothers remember their travels to Uganda, helping conservation projects caring for endangered rhinos, giraffes and lions. And who will the meerkats decide is Disaster Chef when they tuck into Cam and Aaron’s homemade pizza and doughnuts?

programme.title: Gym Stars
programme.desc: The Artistic World Championships are coming to town!

programme.title: The Football Academy
programme.desc: Lola and Charlotte want goals and glory! Jun’ai and Luca get the call-up for a huge under-18s cup game. Lenna Gunning-Williams takes on academy player Lexi in Skill Showdown.

programme.title: Go Get Arty
programme.desc: The Kwapongs get festival-ready as they craft carnival headwear, artist Lakwena Maciver makes a grey rooftop pop with paint, and Abi Clarke draws a pirouetting otter in a tutu!

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Shini meets dogs who help to save lives in the mountains. She discovers how the dogs can find someone completely lost in the countryside and puts their skills to the test in training.

programme.title: DIY Deadly with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: In this unprecedented time of lockdown there has never been a better time to learn about the wildlife around us, and Deadly 60 fans have been sending in their videos, photos and questions for Steve Backshall to answer. In this episode, Steve focuses on our parks and gardens. This green space may look pristine, but for wildlife it’s savannah, jungle and mountains all rolled in to one. And judging by CBBC’s most observant viewers, its packed full of deadly animals! Steve unravels the mystery of some blue eggs in a nest, explains how a spider uses camouflage and trickery to lure in its prey, and tells about a worm that isn’t a worm... it’s a lizard! In a DIY demo, Steve then shows us how to keep hedgehogs warm and toasty during winter by building a hibernation house. But that’s not all. The audience gets their chance to ‘beat Backshall’ in a 60-second quickfire round. The question is, how many can Steve get right before his time is up? We also learn that fox cubs are endearing alarm clocks, and why you wouldn’t want to wander around an Australian garden without a grown-up!

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Bionic ends up dressed as a rabbit when Nitro pranks him at a fake photoshoot, and the rest of the Gladiators have fun behind the scenes as they prank the audience at Sheffield Arena.

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Online sensations Jake and Shauni join the bros for the ultimate game of hide-and-seek, in a real school! Callum then challenges them to protect his pie at all costs!

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: Animated deep sea adventure series. Captain Barnacles and his intrepid band of explorers roam the oceans in search of adventure and fun. The Octonauts help out an albino humpback whale with a nasty sunburn.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: When a giant beetle steals D.O.R.I.S.'s brain, thinking it's a nut, Cosima and Big Lizard give chase through the forest. Big Lizard uses unique skills to rescue poor D.O.R.I.S.'s superbrain.

programme.title: Piripenguins

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck has a job punting tourists along the waterways, but a bored Frog thinks he can do a better job. Thanks to Frog's interference, the pair wind up going on a wild trip down the rapids.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
